theme: the haunted crypt of the drowned curator

story.intro: The door seals behind you. This is the haunted crypt of the drowned curator — or what is left of it. Four mechanisms stand between you and the way out, and the air hums like something in here is still awake.
story.outro: The final mechanism yields. Light floods the chamber as the way out swings wide — you walk free, and the hum falls silent behind you.

puzzles.prompt: The terminal's screen loops one scrambled line, as if someone encoded a warning in a hurry before the lights went out. The coded text reads: "20-8-5 / 22-1-21-12-20 / 18-5-13-5-13-2-5-18-19 / 5-22-5-18-25-20-8-9-14-7"
hints: Every number is a letter in disguise — count your way through the alphabet.
1 is A, 2 is B, on to 26 for Z. Dashes separate letters; slashes separate words.
Converting each number to its letter reveals: "THE VAULT REMEMBERS EVERYTHING".
puzzles.reveal: A hidden face slides open on flickering terminal, exposing a single engraved digit: 9.

puzzles.prompt: A margin note in the curator's ledger reads: "Born in these walls, I answer only when called, and I give back exactly what you gave. Name me to the ledger's lock."
hints: It lives in empty halls and only exists once you make a sound.
Call out in a canyon and it calls back in your own voice.
You hear it when your own words return to you.

puzzles.prompt: The dial array cycles through a run of readings in a strict rhythm, then blinks where the next one should be — someone tuned these dials to a code. The sequence reads: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
hints: The jumps grow — this is not simple addition.
Each value is 3 times the one before it.
Multiply 54 by 3 to get 162.
puzzles.reveal: A hidden face slides open on signal dial array, exposing a single engraved digit: 1.

puzzles.prompt: The pedestal wants a resonance total. Its plaque lists three chords: the first rings at 12, the second 3 lower than the first at 9, and the third at 7. The pedestal accepts only the sum of all three.
hints: Read the plaque again — every chord's value is stated or derivable.
The three values are 12, 9, and 7.
Twelve, nine, and seven together make 28.

puzzles.prompt: The strongbox bears no keyhole — only a small brass counter wheel. Something in here has been keeping inventory of the chamber's contents the whole time. Look around the room itself: add the number of rotting crates to the number of iron lanterns, then multiply by the number of guttering candles — that number is the key.
hints: The lock isn't hiding its clue in words — the answer is standing around you, in the room itself.
Take stock of what furnishes this place: there are 2 rotting crates, and there are 3 iron lanterns, and there are 5 guttering candles.
Counting rotting crates, iron lanterns, guttering candles: (2 + 3) × 5 = 25 — the answer is 25.

puzzles.prompt: Five hollows run across the balance rack, each shaped for one relic. The rack will only settle level when every relic rests in its true place. The 5 pieces are marked: 1 — copper gyroscope;  2 — sealed phial;  3 — black lodestone;  4 — etched tuning fork;  5 — hollow prism. The inscription reads: Sealed phial stands at neither end. Sealed phial stands somewhere to the left of hollow prism. Black lodestone stands immediately to the left of etched tuning fork. Etched tuning fork stands immediately to the left of hollow prism. Enter their true order from left to right as a run of numbers (e.g. "42153").
hints: Anchor the strongest clues first — anything pinned to an end or forced immediately beside a neighbor — and the rest has only one place left to go.
Sealed phial stands in position 2 from the left.
Left to right the true order is: copper gyroscope, sealed phial, black lodestone, etched tuning fork, hollow prism — enter "12345".
puzzles.reveal: A hidden face slides open on relic balance rack, exposing a single engraved digit: 9.

puzzles.prompt: The way out holds one last seal: a 3-digit code. Each solved mechanism in this room surrendered a digit — read them in this order: flickering terminal, then signal dial array, then relic balance rack. Enter the full code.
hints: Every digit is already in the room — go back to what the solved mechanisms revealed when they opened.
Order matters: the digit from flickering terminal comes first, then signal dial array's, then relic balance rack's.
The digits in order are 9, 1, 9 — the code is 919.
